commands.review_summary:
The PR adds opt-in AI-generated session titles, persists `autoTitle`, surfaces derived titles through session list/UI paths, and extends session-scoped model-auth and config metadata.

PR surface: Source +344, Tests +200, Other +12. Total +556 across 33 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: the diff source shows plugin-facing credential steering and a new config schema without a generated baseline update. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused automated repair is appropriate because the remaining blockers are mechanical: preserve the plugin auth boundary, refresh config docs baseline, and rerun targeted validation.

target.title: fix(sessions): auto-generate session titles via AI summarization
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Session titles were added as default-on background summarization, but review found upgrade/privacy risk, missing auth-profile propagation, and generated titles overriding existing subject labels.
- Solution: Make AI session titles explicit opt-in, carry the resolved session auth profile into title generation, and keep existing manual/subject naming precedence ahead of generated titles.
- What changed: Updated the session title generator, conversation label auth lookup, runtime model-auth wrapper, session title precedence, config help text, and focused regression coverage.
- What did NOT change: No dependency changes. Manual session renaming still wins, existing subject labels still win, and title generation still uses the selected session model only when sessionTitle.enabled is true.

Fixes #77165

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** model-generated session titles must be opt-in, use the session-scoped auth profile for the background model request, persist autoTitle, and surface the generated title through the session-list/sidebar data path without hiding manual names or subject labels.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node 24.16.0, local OpenClaw PR worktree. Gateway startup used `pnpm gateway:dev` through the requested dev-gateway script with DeepSeek configured. Title persistence/sidebar proof used the patched OpenClaw session-title generator, session store, runtime model-auth lookup, and `sessions.list` row builder against a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
    OPENCLAW_LOG=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/dev-gateway-rerun.log \
    OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-87643 \
    OPENCLAW_MODEL=openai/deepseek-chat \
    bash /media/vdc/code/ai/Hermes/scripts/dev-gateway-start.sh

  TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
    node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/session-title-runtime-proof.mts

  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts \
    src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts \
    src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts

  pnpm check:test-types
  OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs \
    -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental \
    --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  === GATEWAY READY (20x2s) ===
  [gateway] agent model: openai/deepseek-chat (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openai, phone-control, talk-voice; 10.0s)
  [gateway] ready

  OpenClaw session title runtime proof passed
  local OpenAI-compatible endpoint requests: 1
  auth profile used for title request: openai:default (user locked)
  autoTitle persisted: Invoice Export Duplicate Tax Rows
  sessions.list sidebar derivedTitle: Invoice Export Duplicate Tax Rows
  subject precedence row: Existing Subject Label
  manual displayName precedence row: Manual Sidebar Name

  Test Files  6 passed (6)
  Tests  306 passed (306)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** With `sessionTitle.enabled` omitted, no title generation is scheduled. With `sessionTitle.enabled: true`, the title generator uses the session model and user-locked auth profile, persists `autoTitle`, and `sessions.list` exposes the generated `derivedTitle` for sidebar display. Existing `subject` and manual `displayName` remain stronger than generated titles.
- **What was not tested:** No live browser screenshot was captured; the sidebar behavior was verified through the same `sessions.list` row data consumed by Control UI. The title proof used a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint for deterministic model output; gateway startup was verified separately with the DeepSeek model configuration.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit and local runtime proof
- Target test file: src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts; src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts; src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts; src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
- Scenario locked in: sessionTitle is opt-in, title generation carries the resolved auth profile, runtime model auth receives profile parameters, autoTitle persists into the session store, and subject/displayName remain stronger than autoTitle.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the exact review blockers at the narrow functions that schedule title generation, resolve auth, and compute sidebar title precedence.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The first implementation treated missing `sessionTitle` config as enabled, forwarded provider/model without the auth profile used by the reply, and inserted `autoTitle` before `subject` in the title precedence chain.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered generated title display and model override routing, but not opt-in upgrade behavior, auth-profile propagation, or subject-over-autoTitle precedence.
